markdown:
**TL;DR** — Claude Fable 5 is now permanently included in paid plans after a turbulent export-control saga, and Anthropic shipped ten Claude Code releases in ten days overhauling subagent architecture, `/fork`, and auto mode.

## Anthropic and Claude

- **Fable 5 permanently joins paid plans (July 20)** — After US export controls pulled the model in mid-June, Anthropic trained a new cybersecurity classifier blocking the triggering jailbreak in >99% of cases, restored access July 1, and made it a permanent entitlement for Max/Team Premium at ~50% of standard usage limits. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news/redeploying-fable-5)
- **Fable 5 jailbreak framework published (July 2)** — Anthropic detailed Fable 5's cyber safeguards and proposed industry-standard severity tiers for evaluating jailbreak attempts, framing them as a potential cross-industry standard. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)
- **Claude Code 2.1.207–2.1.217 shipped July 11–21** — Ten releases in ten days; headline changes: `/fork` now spins off a background session copy while you keep working (old behavior moved to `/subtask`); subagents capped at 20 concurrent and blocked from spawning nested agents by default; MCP calls running >2 min auto-background; `EndConversation` tool added to shut down abusive/jailbreak sessions; `auto` mode reached GA on Bedrock, Vertex, and Foundry without opt-in flag; screen reader mode added; fixed a quadratic slowdown in long sessions from message normalization. [source](https://code.claude.com/docs/en/changelog)
- **"The Making of Claude Code" (July 6)** — Anthropic published a behind-the-scenes piece on Claude Code's evolution from internal tool to shipping product. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)
- **Ben Bernanke joins Long-Term Benefit Trust (July 9)** — Former Fed chair appointed to Anthropic's governance board overseeing the company's public-benefit mission. [source](https://www.anthropic.com/news)

## NixOS ecosystem

- **Documentation funding drive launched (July 10)** — The NixOS Foundation is seeking OpenCollective donations to fund dedicated documentation maintainers; the post frankly acknowledges newcomers routinely hit frustrating gaps. [source](https://nixos.org/blog/)
- **26.05 "Yarara" remains the current stable release (ongoing)** — The May/November release cadence means the next stable version won't ship until ~November 2026; no point releases or major nixpkgs announcements surfaced this week from approved sources.

Nothing genuinely new from the NixOS ecosystem in the last 7 days beyond the docs drive.

## AI coding agents and agent frameworks

- **AWS AgentCore reached GA on Amazon Bedrock (week of July 21)** — Declarative agent orchestration runtime handling memory management, error recovery, and knowledge bases, eliminating manual loop coding for common agent patterns. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/this-week)
- **MCP spec finalizing Tasks and Apps extensions (due July 28)** — LangGraph 1.0 integrates MCP tools as first-class nodes, enabling standard-protocol connections to creative tools like Blender and Unreal; the finalization makes MCP a real inter-agent interop layer. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/this-week)
- **Microsoft open-sourced Windows Agent Framework with Aion 1.0** — 14B on-device model with 32K context designed for local tool-calling and sub-agent orchestration on Windows, positioning Microsoft in the on-device agentic race. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/this-week)
- **Zhipu GLM 5.2** released as MIT-licensed open weights with 1M-token context — one of the few frontier-grade permissive models, lowers the barrier for production self-hosted agent deployments. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://chatgptaihub.com/the-big-ai-coding-agents-story-what-july-16-s-news-means-for-developers/)
- **Daring Fireball:** OpenAI consolidated ChatGPT and Codex into one app this week, renaming the original simple chat "ChatGPT Classic" — Gruber notes the resulting interface is more confusing and bloated than before. [source](https://daringfireball.net/)

## Self-hosting and homelab

- **Immich v3.0.3 now distributing via FUTO F-Droid** — The popular self-hosted photo app officially joined FUTO's F-Droid repo, streamlining updates for de-Googled Android setups. *(selfh.st/weekly fetch returned 403; detail sourced from search excerpt)* [source](https://selfh.st/weekly/2026-07-17/)
- **Dawarich adds poster studio** — The self-hosted location tracker's latest release generates custom printable travel maps; notable as a UX-driven feature for a niche app that's been gaining homelab traction. *(selfh.st weekly, same caveat)* [source](https://selfh.st/weekly/2026-07-17/)
- **EU mandates third-party AI assistant parity on Android (July 21)** — European Commission binding measures require Google to grant third-party AI assistants hardware button control, screen capture, microphone/camera, and always-on audio access on par with Gemini; affects any homelab-adjacent Android automation setup. Gruber calls the scope "breathtaking." [source](https://daringfireball.net/)
- **Apple vs. OpenAI trade-secret suit (ongoing)** — Apple sent legal letters to dozens of former employees now at OpenAI; Daring Fireball has the fullest contextual coverage this week for those tracking the AI talent/IP landscape. [source](https://daringfireball.net/)

## Local and on-device LLM inference

- **Ollama v0.32.1 released July 16** (v0.32.0 July 11) — v0.32.0 adds an interactive "Chat, Code & Work" agent experience; Gemma 4 on Apple Silicon now generates tokens ~90% faster via multi-token prediction and automatic tuning; added compute capability 10.0 support for B200-class GPUs. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://releases.sh/ollama)
- **New Ollama registry models: Laguna XS 2.1 and Kimi K2.7 Code** — Poolside's Laguna XS 2.1 (33B/3B active MoE, agentic coding) and Moonshot AI's Kimi K2.7 Code (coding-focused) are the newest notable additions for local deployment. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://www.promptquorum.com/local-llms/top-open-source-models-ollama)
- **NVIDIA Cosmos 3 Edge (4B)** — World model for on-device robotics and physical AI, targeting latency-sensitive workloads without cloud round-trips; pairs naturally with the Raspberry Pi/Jetson homelab tier. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/this-week)
- **Microsoft Aion 1.0 (14B, open-sourced)** — On-device Windows model via the open Windows Agent Framework; meaningful because it's a vendor-backed, open-weight model with native tool-calling, not a community quantization. *(Outside approved set)* [source](https://aiagentstore.ai/ai-agent-news/this-week)
